feed.title: SAP Community - Sustainability
feed.subtitle: Sustainability blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: Inside the Regulatory Content Service: Everything You Can Access
entry.content:
If you’ve ever wondered why your system doesn’t show the newest regulatory phrases, lists, or templates right after we announce “new content available,” you’re not alone. Those announcements mean new content packages are useable via the Regulatory Content Service—not that every installed system is automatically updated. To access all the embedded content, you’ll need to be on the latest release of SAP S/4HANA for product compliance. Below, you’ll find a clear mapping of what content is available today and the specific system versions that deliver it, so you can see exactly what you get now—and what an upgrade unlocks.

  • Starting from SAP S/4HANA OP2021 release, the system consumes new and updated regulatory phrases

  • Starting from SAP S/4HANA OP2023 release, the system consumes listed substance content

  • Starting from SAP S/4HANA OP2023 Feature Pack 3 release, the system consumes new and updated regulatory graphics

  • Starting from SAP S/4HANA OP2023 Feature Pack 3 release, the system consumes new and updated dangerous goods regulations

  • Starting from SAP S/4HANA OP2025 release, the system consumes new and updated product marketability regulations

  • Starting from SAP S/4HANA OP2025 release, the system consumes new and updated safety data sheets regulations

More content is planned to be made accessible in the future. If you have questions, please feel free to leave a comment or message me via LinkedIn.

entry.title: Comment Directly on User Guide for SAP Green Ledger
entry.content:
We’re excited to introduce external commenting for SAP Green Ledger User Guide on SAP Help Portal!

You can now leave comments directly on User Guide for SAP Green Ledger to share feedback, ask questions, or suggest improvements, right where you’re reading. This helps us improve the guide together by capturing your feedback in context and continuously refining the documentation to better support your daily work with SAP Green Ledger.

For more information about documentation feedback on SAP Help Portal, see Giving Documentation Feedback on SAP Help Portal | SAP Help Portal.

At the moment, commenting is only available for the User Guide. It is not yet supported for Integration Guide or Admin Guide. That said, we’d absolutely love to hear your advice and collect more feedback.

We truly appreciate your time, insights, and support as we work together to make SAP Green Ledger documentation even better!

entry.title: New Content Updates for Product Compliance
entry.content:
With the latest Product Compliance release, many new compliance requirement versions have been added to the embedded regulatory content. Among these is the TRGS Occupational Exposure Limits for Germany. To accommodate the additional data included in this compliance requirement version, we have expanded the Manage Compliance Requirement – SDS app.

Embedded regulatory content, by the numbers:

  • 15 Compliance requirements for dangerous goods management
  • 92 Compliance requirements for occupational exposure limits
  • 102 Compliance requirements for safety data sheet management
  • 142 Compliance requirements for safety data sheet authoring
  • 273 Compliance requirements for product marketability
  • 858 Embedded regulatory graphics
  • Over 31,000 Embedded regulatory phrases
  • Over 370,000 Embedded listed substances and other listed substance content

To see which compliance requirement versions are available, check out the SAP Help Portal pages for:

  • Dangerous Goods Content
  • Product Marketability Content
  • Safety Data Sheet Management Content
  • Safety Data Sheet Authoring Content
  • Occupational Exposure Limits Content  

There are also two new read APIs for phrase-enabled fields and compliance requirement versions. These APIs help fill in any gaps in the regulatory content. For example, if you have a phrase imported that only contains the Phrase ID, you can use the read API to retrieve the rest of the information and translations that match the Phrase ID. You can find both APIs on the Business Accelerator Hub here.

A first step toward the vision of allowing users to import and view content which has been brought into the system is here. A new app, Manage Regulatory Content Updates, is available to view information on content imports to your product compliance system from the Regulatory Content Service. This app is currently only available in public cloud systems.

In this demo video you will see setting up a third-party content provider communication arrangement to product compliance for regulatory content as well as a glimpse at the new app. 

You can find the help portal documentation for Communication Scenario SAP_COM_0666 (Product Compliance - Regulatory Content Partner Integration) here.

You can also check on upcoming features in the Roadmap Explorer. For information on all new product compliance features, visit the What's New section of the SAP Help Portal.

entry.title: SBTi Forest, Land, and Agriculture (FLAG) in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management
entry.content:
As companies work towards meeting their climate targets, the distinction between fossil and land-based emissions is becoming increasingly relevant. Emissions from forests, land, and agriculture (FLAG) follow fundamentally different dynamics than those from fossil fuels and materials. FLAG emissions have a significant share in global greenhouse gas emissions, play a critical role in climate mitigation, biodiversity protection, and food system resilience, which provides several advantages in environmental impact mitigation within value chains.

However, these emissions are often bundled together with Fossil GHG Emissions within corporate inventories without the level of detail to credibly manage them. This makes it difficult for companies to set realistic targets, assess progress, or transparently communicate the role of land-based emissions and removals within their GHG inventories. 

When properly understood as part of the natural carbon cycle, FLAG emissions become an important part of net-zero strategies: They are governed by biological processes that allows them to interact directly with removals and carbon storage within shorter timeframes compared to Fossil emissions. 

Against this backdrop, the latest updates to SAP Sustainability Footprint Management mark a significant evolution from a single-impact focus to a holistic environmental footprint analysis. The new capabilities expand impact coverage and explicitly distinguish between fossil and biogenic emissions and removals, enabling companies to reflect land-based dynamics more accurately. 

 Why distinguishing FLAG emissions matters at corporate and product level 

Distinguishing FLAG emissions from Fossil emissions is not just an accounting exercise. At the corporate level, separating FLAG from non-FLAG emissions allows companies to determine whether they are required to set dedicated FLAG targets under certain criteria and to incorporate mitigation pathways that reflect the realities of land-based systems.  

At the Corporate level, this differentiation enables a more realistic target setting, prevents the inappropriate use or double counting of removals within corporate inventories, supports transparent reporting, and progress toward emissions reductions. Without this separation, land use impacts can get hidden behind corporate totals, making progress appear stronger than it actually is.

Within a corporate level inventory, consistently separating FLAG emissions within Scope 3 improves transparency, avoids misleading pictures of progress, and enables more comparable and credible reporting across companies and sectors. To do this, a value chain perspective is needed to ensure all FLAG emissions are accounted across upstream and downstream activities, in order to avoid the risk of them being lost into broader corporate emissions totals, making it difficult to apply removals appropriately or to reflect the distinct mitigation dynamics of land-based systems. This approach is especially important given the limited and evolving guidance on corporate-level inventories for agriculture, forestry, and other land-use (AFOLU) activities.  

At the product level, the distinction becomes even more critical. For many products, particularly agricultural commodities, food, apparel, and bio-based materials, land use change and land management emissions dominate the product's carbon footprint. Without clear FLAG attribution, product claims can become misleading; comparisons across suppliers and materials lose validity, and the climate benefits of bio-based materials or regenerative practices may be obscured. Explicit identification of FLAG emissions  is therefore essential for credible product carbon footprints, meaningful supplier engagement, and alignment between product level claims and corporate transition strategies. 

 What is Science Based Targets initiative framework for Forest, Land and Agriculture (SBTi FLAG) 

The Science Based Targets initiative framework for Forest, Land and Agriculture (SBTi FLAG) guidance provides a dedicated framework for setting science-based targets that address emissions and removals from forests, land, and agriculture, alongside a company’s near-term and long-term climate targets. It clarifies which emissions fall within the FLAG boundary, how land-based emissions and removals should be accounted for, and how companies are expected to demonstrate progress over time in a way that reflects the biological and physical realities of land systems. 

Under the SBTi FLAG, companies are required to set a FLAG-specific target separate from other emissions targets when either of these conditions is met: 

  1. Sector-based requirement: Companies operating in SBTi-designated FLAG sectors, including forest and paper products, food production (animal source), food and beverage processing, food and staples retailing, and tobacco. 
  1. Emissions-threshold requirement: Companies in any sector whose FLAG-related emissions exceed 20% of total gross emissions across scopes 1, 2, and 3. This threshold must be assessed on a gross basis and cannot be netted against removals. 

When a FLAG target is required, companies must report emissions and removals across clearly defined categories that reflect distinct land-based processes: 

  • Land-use change emissions: Refers to CO2 released from carbon stock losses due to the conversion of land between land use categories, such as deforestation for cropland expansion. 
  • Land management emissions: Refers to both CO2 and non-CO2 gases arising from practices within the same land-use category, including tillage, cropping systems, livestock management, and rice cultivation. 
  • Carbon storage and removals: Refers to CO2 removed from the atmosphere through biological processes such as photosynthesis and stored in long-lived biogenic carbon pools such as forest biomass and soils. 

 To ensure transparency and comparability, companies setting FLAG targets are expected to provide disaggregated information that enables meaningful interpretation and review. This includes: 

  •  FLAG emissions and removals reported per commodity and per region 
  •  Separation of land-use change and land management emissions 
  •  Separate reporting of CO2 and non-CO2 emissions from land management activities 
  •  Removals reported by commodity, with further disaggregation across scope 1 and scope 3 categories encouraged 

 These reporting expectations align with the GHG Protocol Land Sector Guidance and the SBTi FLAG Annex submission requirements, supporting consistent and credible treatment of land-based emissions across corporate inventories. 

That’s where our new SBTi FLAG feature comes in. 

 SAP Sustainability Footprint Management for SBTi FLAG 

To facilitate detailed external reporting, especially frameworks like SBTi FLAG that require specific categorization of impacts, SAP's Sustainability Footprint Management introduces a powerful multi-impact category feature. This enables you to map calculated impact category results directly to relevant reporting categories, ensuring alignment with SBTi FLAG requirements. 

For this use case, the integration and calculation setup follow the same procedure as described in our "Multi-Impact Categories in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management" blog post. For SBTi FLAG, the process utilizes your ERP data to automate activity data collection, map to emission factors for the relevant impact categories, and execute one centralized footprint calculation. The critical difference is ensuring that the relevant impact categories for FLAG are selected during the Footprint Inventory setup. 

Here, you'll define the boundaries and specific impact categories to be assessed in your environmental footprint calculation. This step is crucial for FLAG reporting and the following categories must be selected: 

  • Fossil GHG Emissions from Land Management 
  • Biogenic Non-CO2 Emissions 
  • Land Use Change Biogenic CO2 Emissions 
  • Land Management Biogenic CO2 Removals 

Additionally, Climate Change is always calculated as a mandatory impact category in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management; you can also choose to calculate additional categories, like abiotic resource depletion, in parallel with the FLAG analysis. 

For details, see Manage Footprint Inventory Scopes. 

You also need to ensure the inclusion of impact factors for the relevant categories using the Manage Emission Factors app. Each impact category requires a specific emission/impact factor. When populating either the LCA or Simple Template, input a separate entry for each category, even if they relate to the same product, energy source, or business activity, as they are distinguished by distinct Impact Category IDs. 

For SBTi, you must include the following categories are included via data packages: 

  • Fossil GHG Emissions from Land Management (CCFL) 
  • Biogenic Non-CO2 Emissions (CLCN) 
  • Land Use Change Biogenic CO2 Emissions (CLCLU) 
  • Land Management Biogenic CO2 Emissions (CLCM) 
  • Land Management Biogenic CO2 Removals (RELM) 

Climate Change (CLCH) impact factors must also be added, as this category is always mandatory in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management. Consuming apps like Manage Purchase Products Footprints, Model Energy Flows, Manage Waste Footprints, or Manage Business Activities will utilize these factors for relevant calculations. 

For detailed guidance on maintaining emission factors data, see Emission Factors Data. You'll find a table under Impact Categories, listing all categories, including their IDs, dimensions, and indicators. 

Aside from the impact categories being reported, other calculation parameters like master data, energy flow models, and periodic data remain unchanged. This ensures you can effortlessly scale calculations across different impact categories, allowing existing setups to smoothly adapt and include these additions 

For analyzing the calculated footprints, the Footprint Overview app offers a high-level summary of your impact calculation results, while the Corporate Balance app provides a consolidated view of your organization’s environmental performance. The Purchased Products analytics app now allows display of calculated footprints by impact category using a filter option. 

While the initial dashboards emphasize the Climate Change impact category, clicking on them allows for further detailed analysis. See Using the Footprint Overview Application for more information. 

The Corporate Balance app provides an overarching summary of your organization’s environmental performance. For more on viewing organizational emissions, see Using the Corporate Balance Application. 

The Purchased Products analytics app now provides a detailed understanding of the environmental burden associated with specific procured products across all selected impact categories, offering a richer perspective beyond CO2e. For more insights, see Using the Purchased Products Application. 

Map Calculation Results to Reporting Standards 

The Impact Categories from Sustainability Footprint Management are designed to be standard-agnostic, serving as building blocks for supporting multiple reporting standards simultaneously. This versatility, however, requires final results to be mapped to the appropriate reporting categories for the chosen standard. The following table illustrates this: 

 

ID 

SAP Impact Categories for Climate Change Group  

SBTi FLAG  

Reporting Categories 

CLCH 

Climate Change  

Not applicable, always calculated 

CLCF 

Fossil GHG Emissions 

n/a 

CCFL 

Fossil GHG Emissions from Land Management  

Land Management (non-CO2 emissions)  

CLCB 

Biogenic CO2 Emissions from Combustion  

n/a 

CLCN 

Biogenic Non-CO2 Emissions  

Land Management (non-CO2 emissions) 

CLCLU 

Land Use Change Biogenic CO2 Emissions  

Land Use Change Emissions  

CLCLM 

Land Management Biogenic CO2 Emissions  

Land Management (CO2 emissions) 

RELM 

Land Management Biogenic CO2 Removals 

Carbon Removals and Storage  

RECCS 

Technological GHG Removal by Direct Capture of Industrial Emissions  

n/a 

REDCC 

Technological GHG Removal by Direct Air Capture  

n/a 

REPCC 

Product Biogenic CO2 Uptake  

n/a

entry.title: SAP Business AI & Sustainability: WHY
entry.content:
How AI Powers Sustainable Business Transformation

Sustainability is no longer optional—it’s a business imperative. With over 600 global sustainability reporting regulations and growing stakeholder demands, companies must act now. AI is a game-changer, helping businesses turn sustainability challenges into opportunities.

AI’s Impact on Sustainability

  • Emissions Reduction: AI can cut global greenhouse gas emissions by 5-10% by 2030, supporting the 1.5°C climate goal.
  • Healthcare Advancements: AI improves cancer diagnosis accuracy by up to 36.1%, enhancing patient outcomes.
  • Food Waste Prevention: AI-driven inventory optimization could prevent 907,372 tons of food waste annually, saving $2 billion.

How Executives Can Leverage AI

  • CFOs: Automate ESG reporting and risk management for compliance and cost savings.
  • COOs: Optimize supply chains for lower emissions and waste.
  • CPOs: Screen suppliers for ESG compliance and sustainable sourcing.
  • CSOs: Track carbon footprints and embed sustainability across the business.

Overcoming Sustainability Challenges Despite its potential, sustainability efforts face hurdles:

  • Data Silos: Fragmented data slows decision-making.
  • Manual Processes: Inefficient workflows increase errors.
  • Complex Insights: Distributed data makes actionable insights hard to find.

AI helps by automating data consolidation, improving accuracy, and providing real-time insights—freeing teams to focus on impact.

The Bottom Line AI isn’t just a tool—it’s a catalyst for sustainable growth. By integrating AI into sustainability strategies, businesses can drive efficiency, compliance, and positive global impact.

entry.title: SAP Sustainability Footprint Management for the Chemicals (Together for Sustainability)
entry.content:
Chemical value chains are naturally complex. Products are diverse; production routes vary, and materials move through multiple transformation steps across global supply networks. This complexity makes emissions difficult to trace, methodologies hard to align, and results challenging to compare in a meaningful way. 

One of the major obstacles is limited upstream transparency within the value chain. Without consistent supplier-specific data, companies are forced to rely on proxies and averages to determine Product Carbon Footprints (PCFs) which may weaken the credibility of calculations and make effective decarbonization harder to achieve. 

Agnostic carbon accounting standards provide an important baseline, but they were never designed to fully capture the realities of the chemical industry. Methodological choices, such as system boundaries, allocation rules, or data granularity, can significantly influence results, leading to divergent outcomes even when companies follow the same high-level standards. This lack of sector-specific alignment is why initiatives like Together for Sustainability (TfS) were created, to translate generic guidance into a consistent, chemical-industry-specific approach that improves transparency and comparability. 

SAP Sustainability Footprint Management’s new multi-impact capability further supports this sector-specific guidance, by strengthening its alignment with the TfS calculation methodology and reporting categories. Embedding these requirements directly into Corporate Scope 3.1 (Purchased Goods and Services) inventories and PCF workflows and data structures, supports more transparent and comparable PCFs across chemical value chains, helping companies operationalize the TfS guidance at scale and move toward insights driven by high-quality, supplier-specific data. 

What is Together for Sustainability about 

Together for Sustainability (TfS) was created in response to a shared challenge across the chemical sector: sustainability efforts were being slowed down by unaligned approaches to Scope 3 and Product Carbon Footprints. Despite the existence of GHG standards that define the rules for PCF calculation, differences in interpretation and application across companies have led to PCFs and Scope 3.1 figures that are influenced as much by methodological choices as by actual performance. This lack of consistency makes data difficult to compare, exchange, or use with confidence. 

The objective of the TfS Guideline is to address this gap by defining a consistent, sector-specific approach to carbon data collection and calculation for the Chemical Industry. At its core, the guideline establishes clear boundaries and principles for PCF and Scope 3.1 accounting. It provides a uniform process for data collection and emission calculation, and a robust, audit-ready framework that can be applied consistently across companies and suppliers. In doing so, it harmonizes how PCFs are calculated in the chemical sector, reducing variability driven by interpretation instead of real differences. 

A central focus of the guideline is the integration of supplier-specific PCF data into both corporate Scope 3.1 inventories and downstream PCFs. High-quality supplier data is a prerequisite for meaningful cradle-to-gate calculations, enabling chemical companies to link upstream raw materials to their own production processes with better accuracy. This is particularly important in a sector where purchased goods often represent the largest share of total emissions, and where companies have significant influence over sourcing choices. 

How SAP Sustainability Footprint Management supports Together for Sustainability  

To support industry-specific standards like TfS, that include a specific categorization of impacts, SAP's Sustainability Footprint Management introduces a powerful multi-impact category feature. This enables you to map calculated impact category results directly to relevant reporting categories, ensuring alignment with TfS requirements. 

 

 

For this use case, the integration and calculation setup follow the same procedure as described in our "Multi-Impact Categories in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management" blog post. The process utilizes your ERP data to automate activity data collection, map to emission factors for the relevant impact categories, and execute one centralized footprint calculation. To support the TfS use case, it is important to ensure that the relevant impact categories for reporting are selected for the calculation 

Footprint Inventory setup 

In this step, you'll define the boundaries and specific impact categories to be assessed in your environmental footprint calculation according to TfS requirements, in this case, it would be cradle-to-gate. Here, the following categories should be selected, in TfS datamodel Version 3.1 September 2025: 

SAP Environmental Impact Category 

TfS Mandatory/Optional 

Climate Change 

Mandatory 

Fossil GHG Emissions from Land Management 

Optional 

Fossil GHG Emissions 

Mandatory in 2027 

Biogenic Non-CO2 Emissions 

Optional 

Land Use Change Biogenic CO2 Emissions 

Mandatory in 2027 

Land Management Biogenic CO2 Emissions 

Mandatory in 2027 

Land Management Biogenic CO2 Removals 

Optional 

Technological GHG Removal by Direct Capture of Industrial emissions 

Required under Condition 

Product Biogenic CO2 uptake 

Required under Condition 

 

For details, see Manage Footprint Inventory Scopes. 

Emission Factors for Calculation 

Additionally, you also need to ensure the inclusion of impact factors for the relevant categories using the Manage Emission Factors app. Each impact category requires a specific emission/impact factor. When populating either the LCA or Simple Template, input a separate entry for each category, even if they relate to the same product, energy source, or business activity, as they are distinguished by distinct Impact Category IDs. 

For TfS, depending on which impact categories selected, you must include the following categories are included via data packages: 

  • Climate Change (CLCH) 
  • Fossil GHG Emissions (CLCF) 
  • Fossil GHG Emissions from Land Management (CCFL) 
  • Biogenic Non-CO2 Emissions (CLCN) 
  • Land Use Change Biogenic CO2 Emissions (CLCLU) 
  • Land Management Biogenic CO2 Emissions (CLCM) 
  • Land Management Biogenic CO2 Removals (RELM) 
  • Technological GHG Removal by Direct Capture of Industrial Emissions (RECCS) 
  • Product Biogenic CO2 Uptake (REPCC) 

For detailed guidance on maintaining emission factors data, see Emission Factors Data. You'll find a table under Impact Categories, listing all categories, including their IDs, dimensions, and indicators. 

Model Footprint Parameters 

Aside from the impact categories being reported, other calculation parameters like Manage Periodic Data, Energy Flows, and Footprint Parameters remain unchanged but must follow TfS requirements to comply with the standard. 

TfS details specific requirements around allocation of co-products and waste that can be set up on SAP Sustainability Footprint Management. Calculation Parameters such as Allocation rules and factors can be defined in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management under Manage Calculation Variants. This is important in multi-output process as TfS follows a strict allocation hierarchy.   

Transport 

TfS requires upstream transport, internal transport within a company site, and transport of intermediate products between sites to be calculated. Outbound transport to clients is outside the boundary scope. Transportation emissions shall be calculated on a well-to-wheel basis with allocation based on mass or volume. Additionally, aircraft emissions require special consideration to the Radiative Forcing Index. 

SAP Sustainability Footprint Management Manage Transport setting app, allows to setup both supplier and internal transportation; mass allocation is supported. When calculating for TfS, if mode of transport Air is selected, you must ensure that the Emission Factors applied correspondingly take RFI into account when relevant for the calculation. 

Footprint Calculations 

A core principle of TfS is that GHG emissions are calculated by multiplying activity data by the corresponding emission factors (expressed in CO2e per declared unit). 

This is reflected in the Calculate Footprints app which includes all relevant data as defined in the Footprint Inventory Scope, Calculation Variant, and Period. Before starting the calculation, it’s important to verify that the footprint inventory data is complete to ensure accurate results across all selected impact categories. The calculation engine will then process all assigned impact factors against the activity data.  

Analyze Footprints  

For analyzing the calculated footprints, the Footprint Overview app offers a high-level summary of your impact calculation results, while the Corporate Balance app provides a consolidated view of your organization’s environmental performance. The Purchased Products analytics app now allows display of calculated footprints by impact category using a filter option. 

While the initial dashboards emphasize the Climate Change impact category, clicking on them allows for further detailed analysis. See Using the Footprint Overview Application for more information. 

The Corporate Balance app provides an overarching summary of your organization’s environmental performance. For more on viewing organizational emissions, see Using the Corporate Balance Application. 

The Purchased Products analytics app now provides a detailed understanding of the environmental burden associated with specific procured products across all selected impact categories, offering a richer perspective beyond CO2e. For more insights, see Using the Purchased Products Application. 

Map Calculation Results to Reporting Standards 

The Impact Categories from SAP Sustainability Footprint Management are designed to be standard-agnostic, serving as building blocks for supporting multiple reporting standards simultaneously. This versatility, however, requires final results to be mapped to the appropriate reporting categories for the chosen standard. The following table illustrates this: 

ID 

SAP Impact Categories for Climate Change Group  

Together for Sustainability Reporting Categories 

CLCH 

Climate Change  

PCF excluding Biogenic CO2 uptake  

CLCF 

Fossil GHG Emissions 

Fossil GHG Emissions 

CCFL 

Fossil GHG Emissions from Land Management  

Land Management Fossil GHG emissions 

CLCB 

Biogenic CO2 Emissions from Combustion  

n/a 

CLCN 

Biogenic Non-CO2 Emissions  

Biogenic Non-CO2 Emissions 

CLCLU 

Land Use Change Biogenic CO2 Emissions  

Land Use Change GHG Emissions  

CLCLM 

Land Management Biogenic CO2 Emissions  

Land Management GHG Emissions 

RELM 

Land Management Biogenic CO2 Removals 

Land Management Biogenic CO2 Removals 

RECCS 

Technological GHG Removal by Direct Capture of Industrial Emissions  

CCS Technological CO2 Capture 

REDCC 

Technological GHG Removal by Direct Air Capture 

n/a 

REPCC 

Product Biogenic CO2 Uptake  

Biogenic CO2 Uptake 

 

 

This new feature focuses on environmental impact categories stemming from Life Cycle Impact Assessments (LCAs). Therefore, currently, there is no dedicated category for the TfS GWP Aviation emissions (cradle-to-gate), as this is considered part of the Distribution and Storage lifecycle step. The environmental impacts from Aviation emissions can be calculated under the Transport Mode: Air and consequently be included in the PCF under this lifecycle stage.  

Product Carbon Footprint including Biogenic CO2 uptake is derived by combining all relevant impact categories in line with standard requirements at the point of outbound publication of the PCF.  

As part of our ongoing commitment to expanding our capabilities to support TfS, we plan to include Carbon Content attributes in 2026, further enhancing SAP Sustainability Footprint Management to deliver comprehensive well rounded sustainability insights. Stay tuned for these exciting updates!

entry.title: SAP Business AI & Sustainability: WHAT
entry.content:
SAP is leading the way in enterprise management by integrating sustainability into its core business suite, powered by applications, data, and AI. SAP Business Suite isn’t just a collection of tools—it’s a transformation engine that drives continuous innovation. By embedding sustainability into business processes, SAP helps organizations manage risk, achieve compliance, and unlock new value while meeting regulatory and environmental goals.

SAP Drives Sustainability with Applications, Data, and AI

  1. Applications: Embedded Sustainability SAP’s end-to-end business suite ensures that every transaction is part of a unified framework. This standardization allows sustainability data to flow seamlessly across processes like procure-to-pay and record-to-report. By embedding sustainability into core applications, businesses can track, report, and improve sustainability metrics efficiently.
  2. Data: A Single Source of Truth Sustainability data is often fragmented across different systems. SAP’s Business Data Cloud harmonizes this data into one unified model, ensuring consistency and accuracy. This single source of truth enables better reporting, deeper insights, and confident decision-making across all sustainability initiatives.
  3. AI: Intelligent Automation and Insights SAP Business AI goes beyond basic automation by using AI agents to optimize processes in real time. These agents help reduce errors, lower costs, and improve efficiency while ensuring sustainability-aware decisions. AI also enhances predictive insights, helping businesses balance profitability with environmental impact.

Key Sustainability Focus Areas

SAP supports sustainability across four critical areas:

  • Sustainability Management – Helps set strategies, track ESG metrics, and ensure compliance with global standards.
  • Carbon Accounting – Measures and manages emissions while integrating with financial accounting.
  • Sustainable Supply Chain – Ensures transparency, circularity, and ethical sourcing across the value chain.
  • Operational Compliance – Maintains adherence to environmental, safety, and ESG regulations.

Why SAP’s Approach Stands Out

Unlike siloed sustainability solutions, SAP integrates sustainability into the core of business operations. This ensures that sustainability isn’t an afterthought but a strategic driver of growth. By leveraging AI, businesses can automate complex sustainability processes, gain actionable insights, and make data-driven decisions that reduce risk and unlock new opportunities.

entry.title: SAP Business AI & Sustainability: HOW
entry.content:
Sustainability is no longer optional—it’s a strategic imperative. Businesses must integrate sustainability into their core operations to meet regulatory demands, investor expectations, and societal responsibilities. SAP Business AI provides powerful tools to automate processes, enhance decision-making, and accelerate sustainability initiatives.

  1. Embedded AI for Seamless Sustainability Integration SAP’s embedded AI capabilities streamline sustainability management by automating ESG reporting, carbon accounting, and compliance tracking. This reduces manual effort while ensuring accuracy and regulatory alignment.

  2. Custom AI Extensions for Tailored Solutions Developers can build custom AI applications on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) to address unique sustainability challenges, ensuring flexibility and scalability.

  3. Advanced AI Techniques for Smarter Decisions

    • Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG): Combines large language models with business data to generate context-aware insights.
    • Vector Embeddings: Converts complex data (documents, images) into analyzable vectors for precise sustainability analytics.

  4. AI Use Cases Across SAP Sustainability Solutions

    • ESG Reporting: Automate report generation in SAP Sustainability Control Tower.
    • Carbon Accounting: Analyze emissions data in SAP Green Ledger with AI-driven insights.
    • Supplier Compliance: Automate compliance checks in SAP S/4HANA for Product Compliance.

  5. Executive Benefits by Role

    • CFOs: Unify financial and ESG data for better reporting and risk management.
    • COOs: Optimize operations with AI-driven risk prediction and automation.
    • CSOs: Embed sustainability across the business with AI-powered tracking and reporting.

SAP Business AI empowers businesses to achieve sustainability goals efficiently. By leveraging embedded AI and custom solutions, companies can reduce costs, improve compliance, and drive meaningful environmental and social impact.

entry.title: How to Calculate Corporate Carbon Footprint (CCF) in SAP Sustainability Control Tower – End-to-End
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

This is my first blog post on SAP Community, and I’m excited to share my hands-on experience with the Corporate Carbon Footprint (CCF) feature in SAP Sustainability Control Tower.

In this blog, I walk through an end-to-end, practical guide on how organizations can capture, calculate, review, and analyze greenhouse gas emissions using CCF. The focus is on explaining the functional flow, key configuration steps, and how emission results are generated and analyzed within the system.

I hope this guide helps anyone who is getting started with CCF or looking for a clear, structured walkthrough of the feature. If you have any questions or suggestions, please feel free to leave a comment below.

Corporate Carbon Footprint (CCF)

The Corporate Carbon Footprint (CCF) process in SAP Sustainability Control Tower helps organizations calculate their greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions based on business activities across different locations. It uses form-based manual data entry and emission factors to compute and report emissions.

Steps involved in CCF Calculation E2E:

  1. Update master data
  2. Maintain emission factors
  3. Create business activities
  4. Create GHG data collections
  5. Enter data in forms in the collection apps
  6. Review data and send to analytics

 

Update Master Data

Import data into the Legal Entity and Business Location dimensions in the Manage Master Data tile.

When exporting the Business Location template, maintain the ‘Legal Entity’ field to establish a relationship link between the legal entity and the business location.

This relationship is required for the CCF feature.

For further details, refer to:

Update Master Data | SAP Help Portal: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUS_SCT/8aa9148b4141433fa2808f9808b65480/66fd41bbe3a646998146f7ed11f4496d.html?locale=en-US#procedure

 

Maintain Emission Factors

Maintaining emission factors is optional but recommended for accurate carbon footprint calculations. Emission factors can be uploaded via file or entered manually and are used to calculate emissions for business activities.

The Manage Emission Factors app provides default data packages from the GHG Emission Factors Hub (U.S. EPA, DEFRA, GLEC, UBA, EMBER), including:

  1. EPA-2023
  2. EPA-2023-Spend
  3. EPA-2024
  4. UBA – 2022
  5. EMBER_2023
  6. DEFER – 2024

If no custom datasets are available, the default packages can be used. However, they primarily cover Scope 1 (Stationary combustion) and Scope 2 (Purchased electricity, steam, and heat) for the United States. Custom emission factor datasets may be required for other locations or scopes.

For further details, refer to:

Maintain Emission Factors | SAP Help Portal: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUS_SCT/8aa9148b4141433fa2808f9808b65480/06ae39b98a3d4789948e81ab8f4bab65.html

 

Create Business Activities

This app is used to define business activities (in any GHG category) for which emissions data will be manually collected and calculated. Activities are linked to specific locations and associated with emission factors. Users later enter consumption data (for example, fuel used or distance travelled), and the system calculates emissions based on the maintained setup.

Separate activities must be created for each location and activity type.

For example, to track emissions for air travel across your organization, a separate Air Travel business activity must be created for each location.

Let’s see an E2E example with screenshots.

  1. Open the Manage Business Activities tile to create a new activity for calculating emissions at a specific location.


  2. Click the Create button to create a new business activity
  3. Enter the required details. In this example, a new business activity for Air Travel is created under GHG Category Scope 3.6 – Business Travel. The GHG Category field offers a list of available categories along with their supported calculation methods for reference.
    Calculation Methods by GHG Category | SAP Help Portal: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUS_SCT/8aa9148b4141433fa2808f9808b65480/e6790f0245674b89a27397b5d4ae26eb.html


  4. Emission factors can change over time. To reflect these changes, Emission Factor Periods are maintained within the Manage Business Activities app.

    Each period stores updated emission factors and calculation details for a business activity. During footprint calculation, the system uses the emission factor valid for the relevant period, ensuring accurate and time-specific results. Create Emission Factor Periods by clicking on the Create button.
  5. When a new emission factor period is created, it defaults to the activity’s end date. For the next emission factor period, enter the new start date — the system automatically updates the previous emission factor period’s end date.
  6. Under Footprint Details, select the Preferred Footprint Type to define the kind of data users will enter in the Record GHG Data tile.

There are three types:

  1. Manual Emission Footprint
  2. Quantity-Based
  3. Spend-Based

For GHG Category Scope 3.6 – Business Travel, supported calculation methods are Fuel-Based, Distance-Based, and Spend-Based.

  1. Quantity-Based and Spend-Based footprints calculate total CO₂e using emission factor data.
  2. For Manual Emission Footprints, users enter the total CO₂e directly.

In this example, select Quantity-Based footprint type with Fuel-Based calculation method, choosing Manual Entry as the Data Source.

(This is because default emission factor packages are currently only available for Scope 1 and Scope 2.)

After selecting Manual Entry, enter the emission factor:

  1. 2.68 kg CO₂e per liter of fuel.

Note:

  1. The entered value represents the total CO₂e produced per quantity.
  2. The system uses this to automatically calculate emissions based on the quantities that users record in the Manage GHG Data Collections and Record GHG Data apps.
  3. To simplify maintenance, you can use a mass upload feature to import multiple business activity records at once.

For further details, refer to: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUS_SCT/21ecfb83f8d14e5982b7af315da5889a/f8f943508363488ba184965cf4442d46.html

Click Apply to save the details.

Then, click Create button.

 

Create GHG data collections

Use this app to create a periodic data collection form to capture business activity data for each location within a given period.

The Manage GHG Data Collection app helps in recording footprint data linked to business activities. You create a data collection for the relevant period, and users can enter data through GHG data forms, accessible from both this app and the Record GHG Data app

Let’s see an E2E example with screenshots.

  1. After setting up the business activity with the necessary Emission Factor Periods, open the Manage GHG Data Collection app.
  2. Click the Create button to create a GHG Data Collection for the relevant period.  
  3. Then select the Footprint Inventory Scope is “Default (DEFAULT)” and the relevant period. 

     

  4. Then click Create button.

     

  5. After creating the new data collection, it appears in the table with the status "In Progress" and 0% Data Recording Completion. Select the created GHG Data Collection to capture business activity data.
  6. After selecting data collection item in the tables, the GHG Data Collection screen displays general information along with the GHG Data Forms table, which shows all in-scope locations and their data collection progress.

 

Enter data in forms in the collection apps

  1. After selecting a specific location (e.g., Chennai (53002)), you’ll be redirected to the Record GHG Data Form screen, where you can enter GHG data for the selected period and any business activities maintained for that location. Click the Edit button to record GHG Data.
  2. Enter the quantity of fuel used for the business activity at the selected location, then click the Save button to store the data.
    The value 7.01624 represents the total CO₂e emissions generated from the consumption of 2618 liters of petrol. This result is expressed in metric tons, where 1 ton = 1000 kilograms.

    The system used the emission factor of 2.68 kg CO₂e per liter, so:
    2618 liters × 2.68 kg CO₂e/liter = 7016.24 kg CO₂e,
    which is 7.01624 tons CO₂e after converting kilograms to tons.

    This calculation helps quantify the carbon footprint based on the amount of fuel used.

  3. You can mark entire locations or specific business activities as Not Applicable if no data needs to be collected for that specific period. These settings are remembered for future collections, reducing repetitive maintenance.

     
  4. Once all required values are entered, the Data Recording Completion will show 100%, but the status remains In Progress. To finalize the entry, click Set Completed and then Save. The status will change to Completed, and the data becomes available for footprint calculations.

Note: You can still make edits to a form marked as Set Completed or Set Not Applicable by clicking Reopen.

 

Review data and send to analytics

Step 1: Review and Publish GHG Data

Before GHG data can be analyzed or published in the View Emissions Data app, it must be reviewed and confirmed.

  1. Open the Manage GHG Data Collections app.
  2. Select the relevant reporting period and business locations.
  3. Click Review, enter a comment, and click Confirm.

Once confirmed:

  • The Review Status changes to Reviewed.
  • Data becomes available in the View Emissions Data app.

Note: If any data is modified after review, the status updates to Outdated, and a re-review is required.

If the review fails, try again.

Note:
Use the Regenerate option when changes are made that affect an existing GHG Data Collection. For example, if new business activities are created after a data collection has already been generated, regenerating the collection updates the data forms to include those new activities. Previously entered data is retained and not lost during regeneration

Step 2: Analyze Recorded GHG Data

Use the View Emissions Data app to analyze the collected and confirmed data.
The Emissions Dashboard provides insights into:

  1. Gross GHG Emissions
  2. Net Emissions
  3. Emissions Avoided by Renewables
  4. Offsets

This helps monitor trends and evaluate sustainability performance across locations and periods.

Thank you for taking the time to read this blog. I hope this end-to-end walkthrough of the Corporate Carbon Footprint (CCF) feature in SAP Sustainability Control Tower was helpful and provided clarity on how emissions can be captured, calculated, reviewed, and analyzed in the system.

If you have any questions, suggestions, or would like to share your experiences, please feel free to leave a comment below. I’d be happy to engage and learn from the community.

Stay tuned for upcoming blog posts, where I’ll continue sharing practical insights on SAP Sustainability solutions, new features, and technology-focused topics in SAP PaPM Cloud.

entry.title: SAP Sustainability Control Tower: Product Updates (HY2-2025) and Road Map Highlights (HY1-2026)
entry.content:
Introduction

Sustainability reporting is shifting from annual disclosure to continuous, operational performance management, and that requires sustainability intelligence that runs inside the business, not beside it. SAP’s 2026 position is straightforward: sustainability AI must run where the business runs, inside your ERP, or it won’t be truly intelligent, scalable, or manageable.

SAP Sustainability Control Tower supports this by grounding sustainability insights in a trusted enterprise context and embedding them into the end‑to‑end lifecycle of scope definition, governed data collection, analysis, and AI‑assisted reporting, to enable faster, lower‑effort compliance and measurable business impact.

With that framing, the updates in HY2 2025 focus on strengthening the end-to-end path from scope → data → insight → disclosure, and our HY1 2026 roadmap continues to expand regulatory coverage, auditability, data quality controls, and AI-enabled reporting so that customers can achieve faster, lower-cost compliance with measurable business impact.

Q3 & Q4 2025 Highlights for SAP Sustainability Control Tower

Content Factory: Accelerating regulatory metric content at scale

To support customers on their ESRS journey, SAP SCT continues to expand SAP‑provided sustainability metric content, covering both quantitative and qualitative ESRS requirements, while reducing duplication and improving mapping quality.

 

We significantly increased ESRS coverage to help customers meet ESRS data-point requirements, with clear visibility into coverage progress. As communicated in our Help Portal, SAP SCT’s ESRS datapoint coverage and the number of released/planned metrics increased substantially through 2025, with the aim to enable ESRS reporting based on a strong data foundation and sourcing capabilities.

Reporting Scope Management: Turning DMA results into actionable data requirements

ESG data collection relies on a clear reporting scope, usually based on a company’s double materiality assessment (DMA), to determine what to report, when, how, and by whom. SAP SCT’s Reporting Scope features help turn disclosure requirements into specific, system-managed data needs.

With Reporting Scope Management, you can:

  • Capture the outcome of materiality assessments (“what to report”) and map it to SAP-provided ESRS metrics or your own custom metrics.
  • Define scope items across organizational structures and reporting cycles so the scope becomes the backbone for workflows, analysis, and reporting.
  • Ensure traceable collection and reporting over periods to make scope changes transparent and auditable over time.

 

Formula-based metric definition: Configure sustainability KPIs with a flexible Formula Builder

Sustainability reporting rarely ends with out-of-the-box metrics alone. That’s why SCT supports the creation of custom sustainability metrics with the same metadata richness as SAP‑provided metrics, and now with a Formula Builder that makes defining calculation logic far more accessible.

With a formula-based metric definition, you can:

  • Create custom sustainability metrics that are not part of SAP delivery and assign consistent metadata.
  • Define variables mapped to source measures and apply dimension filters (for example, restrict to a specific entity, location, or category).
  • Use the formula builder to define expressions that support arithmetic, conditional, and aggregation logic, helping you align internal KPIs or industry-specific requirements with your reporting model.

Analyze ESG Data app: Personalized insights, drilldowns, and governed access

In HY2 2025, we continued to evolve the analytics experience with the Analyze ESG Data application, a flexible, personalized way to explore both SAP-provided and custom metrics, replacing static KPI tiles with a more dynamic analysis layer.

 Key capabilities include:

  • Drilldown analysis across organizational and metric dimensions (for example, business location, reporting line, legal entity, time period, and predefined metric dimensions).
  • Filtering and grouping by time, hierarchy, sustainability matter, reporting frameworks (like ESRS / EU Taxonomy), or user-defined categories.
  • Personalization: save and share views to align analysis across stakeholders and teams.
  • Governance: instance-based authorization supports secure data access and separation of duties.

Enriched AI-based ESG reporting: Customizable templates + in-app AI actions

SAP SCT’s AI-assisted reporting capabilities continue to mature, focusing on reducing manual effort while increasing control, structure, and reusability. In HY2 2025, we strengthened report generation with:

  • User-defined templates to structure ESG reports and include SAP‑shipped quantitative metrics alongside charts and tables from a pre-shipped list.
  • AI actions for in-app editing, enabling users to refine generated text (e.g., shorten or lengthen it) directly within the report.

This allows sustainability teams to move quickly from data to narrative while maintaining consistent, reviewable reporting results that align with internal disclosure requirements.

Our Initiatives: Linking sustainability ambition to execution

SAP SCT is designed to help organizations move beyond compliance into performance management, including tracking sustainability initiatives with transparency into benefits and costs. Our Initiatives App capabilities focus on turning goals into actionable work with traceability.

With Our Initiatives, organizations can:

  • Create and manage initiatives across lifecycle statuses with detailed metadata (impacted metrics, financials, benefits, actions).
  • Capture CapEx / OpEx, business-as-usual comparisons, and sustainability impact with system-supported calculations and guidance.
  • Break initiatives into actions, assign owners, and track changes to support structured planning and measurable execution.

One API: Unified Sustainability Metrics API for reporting & disclosure consumption

To simplify integration with disclosure tools, reporting platforms, and external consumers, SCT introduced a unified Sustainability Metrics API (One Read API) for metric value consumption.

Key capabilities:

  • Unified retrieval of metric values across quantitative and qualitative metric types.
  • Exposure of metric metadata, including ESG standard references for tagging purposes (important for downstream disclosure).
  • Bulk retrieval based on reporting scope, period, and hierarchy; plus flexibility for time aggregation and hierarchy selection.
  • Ongoing support for current APIs remains, but updates are planned primarily in the unified API going forward.

CPE‑UM value scenarios: extensibility for advanced ESG scenarios

SCT’s value scenarios can be extended with CPE‑UM, which enables read/write access to SCT’s data foundation for advanced customer scenarios.

This helps implement scenarios such as:

  • Sample reporting (read scenario) using a connector architecture for performance and flexibility.
  • Custom metric calculation (read/write) by consuming SCT data and writing back calculated values.
  • Custom data consolidation (write scenario) to operationalize ESG data provisioning into SCT.

 H1 2026 Roadmap Highlights

Forward-looking statement: The following roadmap items are planned and may change.

For HY1 2026, SCT priorities focus on: expanding regulatory coverage, market-ready reporting scope & data collection, enhancing analytics & reporting, and wider adoption & integration options. 

IFRS S1 / S2 coverage (Regulatory expansion & interoperability)

We plan to expand regulatory content beyond ESRS by adopting IFRS S1 and S2 where interoperability exists and by providing preconfigured metric definition content to help customers address investor-oriented baseline disclosure requirements.

Link to Road Map Item

Evidence Management (MVP) for Audit Readiness

To strengthen auditability across the ESG lifecycle, SCT plans an Evidence Management capability (MVP) as a central application to attach, manage, and access documents related to ESG data entries.

Planned core capabilities include:

  • Central repository for attachments linked to reference objects with metadata.
  • Upload/download / metadata updates/deletion, including multiple attachments per object.
  • End-to-end scenario staged across quantitative data and qualitative ESG data, with later integration into data collection forms.

Link to Road Map Item

Data products: Sustainability Metrics (consumption & integration scenarios)

We plan to strengthen “data product”- style consumption and integration scenarios so customers can adopt prebuilt integration paths for key measures (e.g., energy consumption and workforce data), with mapping and low-touch operations.

Link to Road Map Item

DMA process coverage & Reporting Scope enhancements

To operationalize the DMA-to-disclosure pipeline, roadmap items include improvements around reporting scope import/export and interfaces to push DMA lists into reporting scope, plus enhancements in data collection configuration driven by scope.

Link to Road Map Item

Multiple language and format support in AI reporting

Planned enhancements for AI-assisted reporting include:

  • Ability to generate reports in multiple languages.
  • Improved output formats (for example enhanced export options) and more flexible generation options (such as filtering at hierarchy level).

Link to Road Map Item

Custom validation rules & plausibility checks for data import

To improve data quality and reduce the risk of incorrect disclosure, SCT plans configuration options for custom validations and plausibility checks, including rules for value ranges, dimension governance, dependency rules across value combinations, and time-based plausibility checks using historical comparisons and tolerance thresholds.

Link to Road Map Item

Stay tuned for more and check the SCT Roadmap Explorer.

Additional Information

 For in-depth details on SAP Sustainability Control Tower, we recommend browsing the SAP Help Portal. For an overview of all new features and functions, check the What’s New section, which is updated with every new software release.

entry.title: How SAP Business AI Drives Sustainability in Automotive Manufacturing
entry.content:
Sustainability is no longer optional—it’s a business necessity. SAP Business AI helps companies integrate eco-friendly practices by analyzing environmental impact and suggesting sustainable alternatives.

Take Martur Fompak International (MFI), a leader in automotive seat production. MFI’s innovative solution uses SAP AI Core, SAP AI Launchpad, and SAP Sustainability Footprint Management to suggest sustainable fabric alternatives for seat covers. By analyzing their catalog with AI, MFI helps customers and internal teams compare materials based on carbon impact, enabling data-driven sustainability choices. Read the full story here.

Key benefits of SAP Business AI for sustainability include:

  • AI-driven recommendations for sustainable materials.
  • Carbon impact tracking to compare environmental footprints.
  • Seamless integration into business operations for long-term sustainability.

Summary 

  • SAP Business AI helps businesses analyze environmental impact and identify sustainable alternatives.
  • MFI uses AI to suggest eco-friendly fabrics and measure carbon footprints in automotive seat production.
  • SAP Sustainability Footprint Management provides data-driven insights for informed decision-making.
  • AI integration embeds sustainability into core business operations for long-term impact.

entry.title: Championing Ethical and Sustainable AI with SAP
entry.content:
Artificial Intelligence (AI) is reshaping industries, but its rapid growth demands responsible development. SAP is leading the way by embedding ethical principles, security, and sustainability into AI solutions.

Responsible AI at SAP SAP’s approach to AI is built on three pillars:

  1. Ethical and Safe AI – Guided by ten principles, including fairness, sustainability, and human oversight, SAP ensures AI benefits users and society.
  2. Secure AI – Robust security controls protect AI systems from threats, maintaining trust in data and business processes.
  3. Compliant AI – SAP ensures AI solutions meet regulatory standards, helping businesses operate confidently.

Sustainable AI for a Greener Future AI’s environmental impact is a growing concern. SAP minimizes its carbon footprint through:

  • Renewable Energy – Powering data centers with 100% renewable electricity.
  • Optimized Training – Scheduling AI model training during peak energy efficiency.
  • Net Zero Commitment – Aligning AI initiatives with SAP’s 2030 net-zero emissions goal.

By prioritizing ethical, secure, and sustainable AI, SAP ensures technology drives progress without compromising trust or the planet.

Key Points 

  • SAP’s responsible AI focuses on ethics, security, and compliance to build trustworthy solutions.
  • Ethical AI follows ten guiding principles, including fairness and human oversight.
  • Sustainable AI reduces emissions through renewable energy, optimized training, and net-zero goals.
  • SAP ensures secure and compliant AI to protect data and meet regulatory standards.
  • The goal is a positive emissions ROI, where AI avoids more emissions than it generates.
  • SAP earns ISO 42001 certification for AI governance and provisioning, setting the global standard for responsible AI with certified governance across Joule, SAP AI Core, and key SAP Business AI solutions.

entry.title: Welcome to the SAP Sustainability Learning Group
entry.content:
In this space, you can connect with SAP experts and fellow learners across the entire spectrum of corporate sustainability—from ESG reporting to the "Green Ledger." The Sustainability Learning Group is where you will be the first to hear about our plans for introducing new courses, updates to existing sustainability learning journeys, new live sessions, and certification updates.

Our Focus: Learning for a Sustainable Future

The focus of this community is learning and knowledge building. It is not meant to replace existing SAP Community groups that focus on product-specific technical discussions or troubleshooting. Those communities still exist and should always be used whenever you have a technical question or need help with a bug on a specific product feature. There, you are more likely to reach a huge number of developers and consultants who can support your technical implementation.

This learning community is a dedicated space to ask questions and join discussions around SAP Sustainability learning content, such as:

  • Courses and Learning Journeys

  • Certification preparation

  • Live sessions and expert webinars

This is a safe space to ask even the most basic questions as you develop your expertise in sustainability management. We welcome questions related to the exercises and systems used in our official learning journeys. However, please note that this is not the place to seek support for troubleshooting product issues encountered on your own or your customer's live systems.

Empowering Your Sustainability Journey

Always remember: everyone in this group is a learner, just like you. Whether you are a sustainability officer or an IT consultant, don't hold back with your questions. We are here to help you get started and to support you if you run into trouble with topics in the learning content. Look out for blog posts from the experts that will help guide you through the complexities of ESG data.

Solutions Covered in this Community:

The solutions we cover in this community include the core of the SAP Sustainability Portfolio:

  • SAP Sustainability Footprint Management (Product and corporate carbon accounting)

  • SAP Sustainability Control Tower (Holistic ESG reporting and steering)

  • SAP Sustainability Data Exchange (Transparency across the value chain)

  • SAP Responsible Design and Production (Circular economy and plastic taxes)

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud for EHS (Environment, Health, and Safety)

  • Circular Economy & ESG Integration within the SAP Digital Core

Moderated by Experts

The community is moderated by SAP experts who are instructors and learning content developers. This means you are connecting directly with the team who developed the course materials and certifications and who present the live sessions.

entry.title: Positioning SAP Sustainability Solutions: A Guide for Businesses
entry.content:
Sustainability is no longer just a buzzword—it’s a business imperative. Companies worldwide are under increasing pressure to reduce their environmental impact, comply with regulations, and meet stakeholder expectations. We offer a suite of SAP Sustainability Solutions designed to help organizations measure, manage, and optimize their sustainability performance.

Why SAP Sustainability Solutions Matter

SAP’s sustainability solutions provide businesses with the tools they need to:

  • Track and report emissions 
  • Optimize resource usage (energy, water, waste)
  • Ensure compliance with global regulations (e.g., CSRD, SEC climate rules)
  • Drive circular economy initiatives through better supply chain visibility

By integrating sustainability data with core business processes, companies can make informed decisions that align profitability with environmental responsibility.

Key Features of SAP Sustainability Solutions

  1. SAP Sustainability Footprint Management – Helps organizations calculate and reduce their carbon footprint.
  2. SAP Responsible Design and Production – Supports sustainable product lifecycle management.
  3. SAP Sustainability Data Exchange – Enables seamless data sharing across supply chains.
  4. SAP ESG Reporting – Simplifies compliance with evolving environmental, social, and governance (ESG) standards.

Who Should Take the Course?

The Positioning SAP Sustainability Solutions course is ideal for:

  • Sustainability professionals looking to leverage SAP tools
  • Business leaders aiming to integrate sustainability into their strategy
  • IT and ERP consultants supporting sustainability initiatives

Conclusion

As sustainability becomes a key driver of business success, SAP’s solutions provide a structured way to embed ESG principles into operations. By taking this course, professionals can gain the knowledge needed to position SAP Sustainability Solutions effectively and help their organizations achieve long-term sustainability goals.

Ready to learn more? Explore the course here.

entry.title: Comprehensive Overview: Analyze ESG Data Application in SAP Sustainability Control Tower
entry.content:
Disclaimer: This blog post reflects the state of the SAP Sustainability Control Tower as of February 17th, 2026.

The Analyze ESG Data application in SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT) has evolved into a central workspace for exploring and reporting sustainability data. Recent enhancements – such as configurable drilldown dimensions, public analytics configurations, extended period selection, and flexible export options – make it easier than ever for users to navigate ESG metrics, create consistent views, and share insights across teams. This overview summarizes the key capabilities that support faster, clearer, and more collaborative sustainability reporting and provides a practical, user-focused supplement to the SAP Help Portal documentation.

Getting Started: “My Views” & Filters

  • The application requires users to select a reporting period and structure before displaying data, ensuring relevant and targeted analysis:

 

  • Once you have selected the relevant Period and Reporting Structure you can also save this using the “My Views” functionality and set it as default. These views are only visible to you, and don't affect other users.

Manage Overviews: Customization and Collaboration

  • The “Overview” dashboard organizes metrics into groups, which are defined in the “Manage Overviews” section. Users can switch between public and private overviews, edit groupings, and add or remove metrics to tailor the dashboard to their needs.
  • Public overviews allow teams to share standardized metric groupings, while private overviews support individual customization.
  • Metrics can be reordered within groups for better visibility, and the system provides clear feedback when no data matches the selected filters. 

Analytics: Deep Dive, Drill-Downs, and Shared Views

  • Users can access the “Analytics” tab by selecting a metric for a prefilled view or starting from scratch via the analytics tab, supporting both guided and exploratory analysis. 

Option 1: Selecting a metric from the “Overview” page:

Option 2: Select the metric within “Analytics” tab

  • Independent whether you clicked on the metric from the “Overview” tab or you selected it directly within the “Analytics” tab, the metric is visualized with a grouping by “Period” per default (if nothing else is applied).
  • Public and private analytics views allow admins and data managers to predefine drill-down configurations for each(!) metric, which can be set as defaults for all users. This ensures consistency in reporting and facilitates knowledge sharing across teams. 

  • The “Manage Analytics” section provides a centralized location to view and edit all saved analytics configurations, with public views editable by authorized users. Any public view can be edited by anyone with the required authorizations.

Advanced Filtering, Drill-Downs, and Export

  • Users can filter and drill down by multiple dimensions, such as period, reporting structure, and energy source type, with up to five drill-downs per metric. This flexibility supports detailed analysis and custom reporting. 

  • Within the “View Settings” you can also create additional filters by “Period”, “Reporting Structure” or “Drill-down Dimension”. Please note that filters within “View Settings” overwrite settings in “My Views” if applied. For filtering within “View Setting” there is a current limit of 12 nodes for multi-selection in hierarchies, set for performance and visualization reasons. Depending on customer feedback this limit might be expanded. 

  • If filtering is applied via the “View Settings” and “My Views” is overwritten, this is also highlighted as an info:

  • Export functionality enables users to download chart data as PNG images and tables as Excel files, making it easy to share insights and integrate with other reporting tools. 

  • Drill-downs for each metric can be set up in the “Manage Metrics” application for up to 5 drill-down dimensions. Select the relevant drill-down dimension(s) and by clicking on saving, this directly calculates the drill downs. Once it has been calculated and saved, the drill-downs can then directly be visualized in the “Analytics” tab of the Analyze ESG Data application

Upcoming Features & Further Resources

  • Key priorities for upcoming releases include displaying targets within the “Analytics” tab, enabling multi-selection in the global filter for reporting structures, support for more chart types with saved custom chart views and saving “Analytics” settings for the whole page across multiple charts further enhancing flexibility and usability. 
  • If you are driving a project with SCT, please upvote or submit improvement requests via SAP Influence to ensure that future updates align with real-world needs and use cases. 
  • For further details, consult the SAP Help Portal and the SAP Roadmap for SCT:
    • Using the Analyze ESG Data Application | SAP Help Portal
    • SAP SCT Roadmap
    • Don’t forget to subscribe the What’s New section within SAP Help:
      What's New in SAP Sustainability Control Tower | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Administering Sustainability Control Tower
entry.content:
Why Administration Matters

Before organizations can effectively manage sustainability data, they need a solid technical foundation. SAP Sustainability Control Tower requires proper setup, role management, and system integration to ensure accurate, consistent, and scalable operations.

A well-administered system enables:

  • Reliable data integration across SAP landscapes
  • Secure and structured user access
  • Smooth operation for business users
  • A strong foundation for reporting and steering sustainability

What You’ll Learn in This Learning Journey

This compact, 1.5-hour course focuses on the essential administrative tasks required to get SAP Sustainability Control Tower up and running:

  1. Setting Up Accounts and Services
    Learn how to manage accounts and services to prepare the system for use.
  2. Assigning Roles
    Understand different user roles and how to assign them to ensure proper access control and governance.
  3. Configuring System Integration
    Connect SAP Sustainability Control Tower with key systems, including:
  • SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • SAP Datasphere
  • SAP EHS Management
  • SAP Sustainability Footprint Management
  • SAP Analytics Cloud

This ensures seamless data exchange and a fully connected sustainability landscape.

Prerequisite: Basic knowledge from the Learning Journey Reporting and Steering Sustainability in Your Businesses using SAP Sustainability Control Tower.

Who Should Take the Course?

This learning journey is designed for:

  • System Administrators responsible for setup and configuration

Build the technical foundation for effective sustainability management and start the Learning Journey “Administering SAP Sustainability Control Tower” here.

entry.title: How to Get Started with SAP Sustainability Data Exchange
entry.content:
Why SAP Sustainability Data Exchange Matters

Sustainability doesn’t stop at company boundaries — it extends across the entire supply chain. To truly reduce environmental impact, organizations must collaborate and exchange reliable data with their partners.

SAP Sustainability Data Exchange enables businesses to securely share product carbon footprint data, improving transparency and accelerating decarbonization efforts.

With SAP Sustainability Data Exchange, companies can:

  • Collaborate with partners on sustainability data
  • Increase transparency across the supply chain
  • Standardize and share product carbon footprint information
  • Support data-driven decarbonization initiatives

What You’ll Learn in This Learning Journey

This short, 1-hour 22-minute course introduces the key concepts and practical steps to start using SAP Sustainability Data Exchange:

  1. Introduction and Business Challenges
    Understand supply chain sustainability challenges and how SAP Sustainability Data Exchange enables collaboration between business partners.
  2. Leveraging Networks
    Learn how the solution integrates with networks like:
  • SAP Business Network
  • Catena-X
  1. Subscription and Setup
    Learn how to provision and adapt  SAP Sustainability Data Exchange within your SAP Business Technology Platform environment.
  2. Getting Started with Data Exchange

      Learn how to:

  • Maintain master data for products and business partners
  • Exchange product carbon footprint data
  • Understand end-to-end carbon management scenarios with SAP Sustainability Footprint Management

Who Should Take the Course?

This learning journey is ideal for:

  • Consultants supporting sustainability and SAP implementations

No prerequisites are required, making it a great starting point for beginners.

Start collaborating on supply chain sustainability today and explore the Learning Journey “Getting Started with SAP Sustainability Data Exchange” here.

entry.title: How to Set Up SAP Sustainability Data Exchange
entry.content:
Why Setup and Integration Matter

To enable effective data exchange across the supply chain, systems must be properly connected. SAP Sustainability Data Exchange relies on strong integrations to ensure that product carbon footprint data flows securely and consistently between partners and platforms.

A well-configured setup enables:

  • Accurate and automated data exchange
  • Reliable integration across sustainability solutions
  • Scalable collaboration with business partners
  • Consistent and trustworthy sustainability reporting

What You’ll Learn in This Learning Journey

This short, 16-minute course focuses on the essential integration setup for SAP Sustainability Data Exchange:

  1. Integration Capabilities and Setup
    Learn how to configure technical integrations between SAP Sustainability Data Exchange and key systems, including:
  • SAP Sustainability Footprint Management
  • Catena-X 

This ensures seamless data exchange and accurate sustainability reporting across your ecosystem.

Who Should Take the Course?

This learning journey is designed for:

  • Consultants supporting sustainability and integration scenarios
  • Administrators responsible for system setup and connectivity

Prerequisite: Basic knowledge from the Learning Journey Getting Started with SAP Sustainability Data Exchange.

Enable seamless sustainability data exchange and start the Learning Journey “Setting Up SAP Sustainability Data Exchange” here.

entry.title: How to Establish Corporate Carbon Footprint Emissions with SAP
entry.content:
Why Carbon Footprint Management Matters

Measuring emissions is essential for any sustainability strategy. Without accurate data, organizations cannot effectively reduce their environmental impact or meet regulatory requirements.

SAP Sustainability Footprint Management enables companies to calculate carbon emissions across their operations, from individual activities to the entire corporate footprint.

With this solution, businesses can:

  • Calculate emissions across GHG Scope 1, 2, and 3
  • Gain transparency into their environmental impact
  • Support regulatory reporting and compliance
  • Build a foundation for decarbonization initiatives

What You’ll Learn in This Learning Journey

This two-hour course provides a comprehensive introduction to corporate carbon footprint management:

GHG Protocol and Regulations

Understand the fundamentals of the Greenhouse Gas Protocol and key regulatory frameworks.

Introduction to SAP Sustainability Footprint Management

Explore the features, capabilities, and architecture of the solution and how it supports carbon tracking across products, operations, and value chains.

System Setup and Configuration

Learn how to set up SAP Sustainability Footprint Management in your SAP Business Technology Platform environment.

Managing Emissions Data

Maintain source data and prepare it for accurate emissions calculation.

Calculating Carbon Footprints

Calculate emissions across Scope 1, Scope 2, and Scope 3 and analyze the results.

Integrating with Corporate Steering

Share calculated emissions data with SAP Sustainability Control Tower to support reporting and strategic decision-making.

Who Should Take the Course?

This learning journey is ideal for:

  • Business Users working with sustainability and reporting
  • Consultants implementing carbon management solutions
  • Administrators responsible for setup and configuration

No prerequisites are required, making it suitable for beginners.

Start building a solid foundation for carbon management and explore the Learning Journey “Establishing Corporate Carbon Footprint Emissions with Simple Configuration” here.

entry.title: What an event! Where else can such a community come together?!
entry.content:
Last week, from March 24–25, the SAP EHS & Product Compliance community met in person in Amsterdam at the SAP EHS & Product Compliance Conference hosted by TAC Insights. The atmosphere was fantastic, and the networking within the community was incredibly lively!

For many, the event began already on Monday evening with partner and customer dinners, as well as preparations for the conference and the partner exhibition hall.

On Tuesday morning, with 220 attendees, the main conference room was packed for our opening keynote. SAP’s Gunther Rothermel shared more about the new generation of agentic processes for operational sustainability—also highlighted in his LinkedIn post.

The agenda continued in this manner, featuring experts who covered EHS and Product Compliance capabilities and innovations, including insights from experienced customers, partners, and SAP specialists.

Across both days, we heard real-world implementation stories, with customers sharing their S/4HANA transformation journeys within the dedicated EHS and Product Compliance tracks. Particularly fascinating were the side-by-side and hybrid approaches customers are adopting to kick-start their transformations instead of waiting for a “perfect moment.” Another strong theme that came through was the added value our partners bring to customer projects by filling gaps and extending what’s possible with our solutions.

As expected, AI was a major recurring topic—not just in terms of future potential, but with concrete demos from SAP and partners. Attendees got a glimpse of how AI can support their everyday work. This theme also shaped a panel discussion on how AI will influence future EHS and Product Compliance processes.

Another highlight was the Discovery Hub. Attendees had the chance to get hands-on experience with the solutions, following trial tours through dangerous goods and compliance document management processes in the Product Compliance room, as well as AI-assisted compliance management and AI-powered Safety Instruction Generation for Risk Assessment in the EHS room. Our SAP experts were kept very busy supporting visitors and discussing scenarios in depth.

Whether during breaks in the exhibition hall at partner or SAP booths, at the evening BBQ, or in the interactive sessions throughout the days—there was always something happening. Peers had countless opportunities to exchange ideas on topics affecting their day-to-day work. Despite the professional focus, the atmosphere remained friendly, honest and open. I’m already looking forward to seeing everyone again!

And that brings me to what’s coming next. Please save the dates:

  • SAP EHS & Product Compliance Virtual Infodays
    September 29 – October 1, 2026
    More information and invitations will follow soon.
  • SAP EHS & Product Compliance Conference presented by TAC Insights
    March 23–24, 2027 in Berlin, Germany
    Visit the event page for details.

 If you’d like to hear more or are interested in contributing to any of the above, feel free to contact us at product.compliance@sap.com.
